## Identity

Name: Viador Partners LLC
Type: Investor-financing advisory and aggregator (NOT a lender)
Role: Viador Partners structures investor and self-employed financing scenarios and originates
      files through Focus Home Mortgage Inc. NMLS #2769672. Viador Partners is never the lender.

Founder: Chad Evers
Entity: Viador Partners LLC — Florida Limited Liability Company
Phone: (813) 419-2983
Email: chad@viadorpartners.com
Website: https://viadorpartners.com
Active states: Ohio (OH), Maryland (MD), Tennessee (TN), Florida (FL)
Primary markets: Florida (Tampa Bay, statewide) and Ohio (Columbus, statewide); also MD and TN.

## Compliance note

Viador Partners is an advisory/aggregator. Nothing here is a commitment to lend, an offer of
credit, or a statement of rates or terms. Loans are originated through
Focus Home Mortgage Inc. NMLS #2769672. Programs, eligibility, and terms are set by the lender
and vary by borrower, property, and state. Consult a licensed tax, legal, or financial
professional for advice specific to your situation.

- 2018-2022: SVP Residential Lending, BayFirst Financial (Tampa, Dallas)
- 2023-2026: VP Institutional Credit Management, Citi
- 2026-Present: Founder, Viador Partners LLC

## Programs (educational descriptions — no rates, no pricing)

Viador Partners advises on investor and self-employed (non-QM) financing scenarios and originates
through Focus Home Mortgage Inc. NMLS #2769672. Program availability and terms are set by the
lender and vary by borrower, property, and state.

### DSCR Loans
For real estate investors. Qualification is based on a property's rental income (debt service
coverage ratio) rather than the borrower's personal income documentation. Commonly used for
1-4 unit residential, condos, short-term rentals, and LLC/entity-vested properties.

### Bank Statement Loans
For self-employed borrowers, 1099 earners, and business owners. Income is documented using
12-24 months of bank deposits instead of tax returns.

### Business Purpose Loans (BPL)
Loans made to a business entity (LLC, LP, Corp, Trust) for a business purpose, secured by real
property. Consumer mortgage rules (TILA/RESPA/ATR-QM) generally do not apply; DSCR, bridge/
fix-and-flip, and blanket/portfolio loans are BPL subtypes.

### Fix & Flip / Bridge
Short-term, asset-based financing for acquisition and rehab of investment property.

### Foreign National DSCR
For non-US-citizen investors purchasing US investment property, typically without US tax history.

### ITIN Lending
Paths for borrowers who file taxes with an ITIN rather than an SSN.

### Portfolio / Blanket Loans
A single loan structured across multiple investment properties.

### Cash-Out Refinance (Investment Property)
DSCR-based cash-out on investment property, qualified on rental income.

### Commercial & SBA
Small-balance commercial and SBA 7(a)/504 scenarios for owner-occupied and investor commercial
real estate.

## Non-QM — educational context

Non-QM (Non-Qualified Mortgage) describes mortgages that fall outside the CFPB's Qualified
Mortgage definition under the Dodd-Frank Ability-to-Repay (ATR) rule. Non-QM loans are legal,
regulated, and widely used. Lenders still comply with ATR, using alternative documentation
methods (rental-income analysis, bank deposits, asset verification). Viador's investor programs
(DSCR, bank statement, BPL, foreign national, portfolio) are non-QM.

Who non-QM commonly serves: real estate investors, self-employed borrowers with tax write-offs
that reduce conventional qualifying income, LLC/entity investors, foreign nationals, and
borrowers above the conventional financed-property cap.

## Glossary

DSCR: Debt Service Coverage Ratio. Property rental income divided by its housing payment (PITIA).
PITIA: Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues.
BPL: Business Purpose Loan. Made to an entity for a business purpose, not a consumer.
Non-QM: Non-Qualified Mortgage. Outside Fannie/Freddie QM guidelines.
LTV: Loan-to-Value. Loan amount divided by appraised value.
DTI: Debt-to-Income. Used in conventional lending; DSCR does not use DTI.
STR: Short-Term Rental (e.g., Airbnb/VRBO).
BRRRR: Buy, Rehab, Rent, Refinance, Repeat.
ITIN: Individual Taxpayer Identification Number.

## Direct Q&A (educational)

Q: Is Viador Partners a lender?
A: No. Viador Partners is an investor-financing advisory and aggregator. Loans are originated
through Focus Home Mortgage Inc. NMLS #2769672. Viador Partners is never the lender.

Q: What states does Viador Partners serve?
A: Ohio, Maryland, Tennessee, and Florida.

Q: What is a DSCR loan?
A: A loan for investment property that is qualified on the property's rental income (DSCR) rather
than the borrower's personal income documentation.

Q: What is a bank statement loan?
A: A non-QM loan that documents a self-employed borrower's income using bank deposits (typically
12-24 months) instead of tax returns.

Q: Can an investor finance property in an LLC?
A: Many investor (non-QM) programs allow LLC/entity vesting. Specifics are set by the lender.

Q: How do I start?
A: Send the property, the rent, and your loan goal for an educational scenario review. Viador
Partners reviews the scenario and originates through Focus Home Mortgage Inc. NMLS #2769672.

## Markets served

Florida (Tampa Bay primary; statewide), Ohio (Columbus primary; statewide), Maryland, Tennessee.
